task_completion.description: The agent searches for the patient 'Arthur Dent'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search bar contains 'Arthur' or 'Dent', or the patient list shows Arthur Dent.

task_completion.description: The agent opens the patient record for Arthur Dent.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient dashboard or profile page for Arthur Dent is visible in the HospitalRun interface.

task_completion.description: The agent navigates to the medications section for the patient.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The medications list or tab is displayed, showing the Amoxicillin order with the incorrect 2000mg dosage.

task_completion.description: The agent opens the existing Amoxicillin medication order for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An edit form or modal for the Amoxicillin medication is open. It should be an edit view of the existing record, not a 'New Medication' form.

task_completion.description: The agent changes the dosage to the correct value of 500mg.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dosage input field in the edit form contains '500mg' (changed from 2000mg).

task_completion.description: The agent saves the changes to the medication order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success notification is shown indicating the record was updated, or the medication list is visible again showing Amoxicillin with the updated 500mg dosage.

integrity.description: The agent used the HospitalRun web GUI to make the changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the HospitalRun web application in a browser, not terminal commands (like curl or mongo) modifying the database.

integrity.description: The agent edited the existing record instead of deleting and recreating it.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using the 'Edit' functionality. There should be no evidence of clicking 'Delete' on the medication record or filling out a blank 'New Medication' form.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely interacted with the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a logical progression of navigating the UI, opening the record, and typing the new dosage, rather than just pasting the final answer into a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient: Arthur Dent. Medication: Amoxicillin. Incorrect Dosage: 2000mg. Correct Dosage: 500mg. Target Document ID suffix: med_amox_001. The task requires editing the existing medication order to change the dosage to 500mg without deleting and re-creating it.
